headline: The Magnificent Seven Fell 40% While the S&P Dropped 18%: Here’s How Much Tech Concentration You Can Actually Afford
summary: I’ve been studying portfolio concentration for years, and one stat from Schwab’s On Investing podcast keeps echoing in my head. In 2022, the Magnificent Seven fell roughly 40% while the S&P 500 dropped 18%. Our data confirms the pain: SPY shed 20% that calendar year, while Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) collapsed 64%, NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) fell 51%, ... headline: Microsoft Reshapes LinkedIn And AI Strategy While Shares Screen As Undervalued
summary: Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) is planning layoffs at LinkedIn of about 5% of staff as part of a global restructuring aimed at higher growth business areas. The company is also adjusting its AI approach by pursuing acquisitions of AI startups and revising its OpenAI partnership to capped revenue and non-exclusive terms. Both moves highlight changes in Microsoft's workforce strategy and its approach to AI investment and partnerships. For investors watching Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT), these updates...

headline: Marvell Stock Pops. Why Wall Street Sees Upside.
summary: While chip makers Intel and Micron have been getting all the love in recent days,  Marvell  got a nice bounce after analysts at BofA and Goldman Sachs raised their price targets for it.  Marvell shares closed up 8.2% to $177.95 on Wednesday, for a new record high.  The stock was up a further 4.2% to $185.40 in premarket trading Thursday.

content.title: Marvell Stock Pops. Why Wall Street Sees Upside.
content.summary: While chip makers Intel and Micron have been getting all the love in recent days,  Marvell  got a nice bounce after analysts at BofA and Goldman Sachs raised their price targets for it.  Marvell shares closed up 8.2% to $177.95 on Wednesday, for a new record high.  The stock was up a further 4.2% to $185.40 in premarket trading Thursday.

headline: Denmark Data Center Market Investment Analysis Report 2026-2031: $3.19 Bn Growth Opportunities Across 33 Existing, 11 Upcoming Facilities, and 14+ Locations
summary: Denmark's data center market is poised to hit USD 3.19 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 8.52% from 2025 to 2031. Key highlights include approximately 33 operational colocation centers, mainly Tier III certified, with Copenhagen as the primary hub hosting 21 centers. Data center construction costs in Denmark range between $10-$12 million per MW, making Denmark a competitive choice compared to other Nordics. The Danish government's tax incentives attract global players like Prime Data Centers

Key highlights include approximately 33 operational colocation centers, mainly Tier III certified, with Copenhagen as the primary hub hosting 21 centers. Data center construction costs in Denmark range between $10-$12 million per MW, making Denmark a competitive choice compared to other Nordics. headline: Southeast Asia Data Center Construction Industry Outlook Report 2026: Investments are Expected to Grow at a CAGR of 17.83% During 2025-2031, Driven by Attraction from Major Operators
summary: The Southeast Asia data center construction market is projected to expand significantly, with an estimated CAGR of 17.83% between 2025 and 2031. headline: UK opens antitrust probe into Microsoft's business software
summary: Britain launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft's dominance in business software that could ‌lead to targeted action if the U.S. company ‌is found to have "strategic market status" in the sector.  It ‌would also look ⁠at how AI competitors were able to integrate with Microsoft's business software, it said.  An SMS ⁠designation would also allow the CMA to intervene in the cloud market, where it has previously found Microsoft's use of ​software licensing ​could be reducing competition ​in cloud services.

content.title: UK opens antitrust probe into Microsoft's business software
content.summary: Britain launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft's dominance in business software that could ‌lead to targeted action if the U.S. company ‌is found to have "strategic market status" in the sector.  It ‌would also look ⁠at how AI competitors were able to integrate with Microsoft's business software, it said.  An SMS ⁠designation would also allow the CMA to intervene in the cloud market, where it has previously found Microsoft's use of ​software licensing ​could be reducing competition ​in cloud services.
